classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XVII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Defilers of themselves with beasts, being also leprous, who have infected others [with the leprosy of this crime], the holy Synod commands to pray among the hiemantes.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: A leper who goes in to a beast or even to leprous women, shall pray with the hybernantes.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Λεπρώσαντας is from λεπρόω not from λεπράω and therefore cannot mean “have been lepers,” but “have made others rough and scabby.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is only in the passive and in Alexandrian Greek that it has the meaning to become leprous.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: There seems but little doubt that the word is to be understood spiritually as suggested above.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The last word of the canon is also a source of confusion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Both Beveridge and Routh understand by the χειμαζόμενοι those possessed with devils.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Suicer however (Thesaurus) thinks that the penitents of the lowest degree are intended, who had no right to enter the church, but were exposed in the open porch to the inclemencies (χειμών) of the weather.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: But, after all it matters little, as the possessed also were forced to remain in the same place, and shared the same name.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Besides the grammatical reason for the meaning of λεπρώσαντας given above there is another argument of Hefele’s, as follows:

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is clear that λεπρώσαντας cannot possibly mean “those who have been lepers”; for there is no reason to be seen why those who were cured of that malady should have to remain outside the church among the flentes.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Secondly, it is clear that the words λεπροὺς ὄντας, etc. are added to give force to the expression ἀλογευσάμενοι.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The preceding canon had decreed different penalties for different kinds of ἀλογευσάμενοι.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: But that pronounced by canon xvii. being much severer than the preceding ones, the ἀλογευσάμενοι of this canon must be greater sinners than those of the former one.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: This greater guilt cannot consist in the fact of a literal leprosy; for this malady was not a consequence of bestiality.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: But their sin was evidently greater when they tempted others to commit it.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is therefore λέπρα in the figurative sense that we are to understand, and our canon thus means; “Those who were spiritually leprous through this sin, and tempting others to commit it made them leprous.”
